chapter_context.summary: The Philistines prepare for war; Saul, terrified and receiving no answer from the LORD, consults a medium at En-dor to summon Samuel.

outline.text: Philistines Muster; Achish Makes David His Bodyguard
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Philistines Muster; Achish Makes David His Bodyguard (28:1-2), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

outline.text: Saul's Terror; God Refuses to Answer Him
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Saul's Terror; God Refuses to Answer Him (28:3-6), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

outline.text: Saul Disguises Himself; Consults Medium of En-dor
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Saul Disguises Himself; Consults Medium of En-dor (28:7-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

outline.text: Samuel's Spirit Pronounces Doom and Rejection on Saul
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Samuel's Spirit Pronounces Doom and Rejection on Saul (28:15-19), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

outline.text: Saul's Collapse and Last Meal at En-dor
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Saul's Collapse and Last Meal at En-dor (28:20-25), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

key_themes.title: Sovereignty of God
key_themes.description: God raises up the humble (Hannah, Samuel, David) and humbles the proud (Eli's sons, Saul).

key_themes.title: The Heart vs. Outward Appearance
key_themes.description: God looks at the inner spiritual character, not physical stature or social status.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 28, how does this encourage you to trust God when circumstances look bleak?
How does this chapter prompt you to examine the sincerity of your own heart before the LORD?

cultural_historical.title: Transition to Monarchy
cultural_historical.text: Israel's request for a king represents a major cultural shift from a tribal league led by temporary judges to a centralized dynastic monarchy.
